Vulnerabilities and Exposures database. As part of our ongoing effort to mature the official

items.reason: Security‑focused article about correcting Kubernetes CVE records; a core topic for a technical wiki and not covered yet.

items.title: Announcing etcd 3.7.0-beta.0
items.summary: <p>SIG-Etcd announces the availability of the <a href="https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/releases/tag/v3.7.0-beta.0">first beta release of etcd v3.7.0</a>. This new version of the popular distributed database and key Kubernetes component includes the long-requested RangeStream feature, as wel
items.reason: Announces a new etcd beta release, a critical component of Kubernetes clusters; warrants its own entry.

May 2026.</em></p>
<p>Kubernetes v1.36 introduces a new alpha counter metric <code>route_controller_route_sync_total</code>
to the Cloud Controller Manager (CCM) route c
items.reason: Introduces a new metric in the Cloud Controller Manager; relevant for monitoring and observability documentation.

items.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Mixed Version Proxy Graduates to Beta
items.summary: <p>Back in Kubernetes 1.28, we introduced the <code>Mixed Version Proxy (MVP)</code> as an Alpha feature (under the feature gate <code>UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy</code>) in a <a href="https://kubernetes.io/blog/2023/08/28/kubernetes-1-28-feature-mixed-version-proxy-alpha/"
items.reason: Mixed Version Proxy graduating to beta is a notable feature change in Kubernetes networking.

items.title: Mitigating CVE-2026-31431 (“Copy Fail”) in Docker Engine
items.summary: CVE-2026-31431 is a Linux kernel vulnerability that was recently disclosed. This CVE does not compromise Docker infrastructure. That said, Docker Engine's default profiles prior to v29.4.3 allowed containers to create AF_ALG sockets, which is the syscall surface the exploit uses. You are not exposed
items.reason: Docker CVE mitigation guide addresses a recent kernel vulnerability; essential security content.

items.title: The Untrusted Autonomous Workload: How AI Coding Agents Reshape What Isolation Has to Do
items.summary: Earlier this year I mass-migrated my blog to Astro using Claude Code. 146 posts. 6,024 images. Canonical URLs, JSON-LD markup, sitemap generation, the whole stack. I'd spent hours writing a skills file to teach the agent about my blog's architecture, how deployment worked, what not to touch. And it
items.reason: Explores AI coding agents and container isolation—a emerging topic that fits the wiki’s AI‑in‑DevOps scope.

items.title: Meet Gordon: Docker’s AI Agent For Your Entire Container Workflow
items.summary: Gordon understands your environment, proposes fixes, and takes action across your entire Docker workflow. Now generally available. Image 1: Gordon in Docker Desktop Why Gordon Exists  Developers are more productive than ever. AI coding assistants are writing code, merging PRs and cutting review
items.reason: Launch of Docker’s AI agent “Gordon” introduces a new workflow tool; worth documenting.

AI coding agents are everywhere. According to Anthropic's 2026 Agentic C
items.reason: Security‑focused series on coding‑agent failures aligns with the wiki’s security and AI sections.

items.title: Custom MCP Catalogs and Profiles: Advancing Enterprise MCP Adoption
items.summary: We’re excited to announce the general availability of Custom Catalogs and Profiles for managing Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. These two complementary capabilities fundamentally change how teams package, distribute, and manage AI tooling.  Custom MCP Catalogs let organizations curate and dist
items.reason: Custom MCP Catalogs and Profiles are new enterprise‑level features for AI tooling; relevant for AI‑ops documentation.

items.title: Still a developer. Just outside. Our latest GitHub Shop collection is here.

The ESC collection lets you escape the confines of your desk and get out into the sun where good ideas are bound to happen.

### 🐛 Fixes
